# Unmuze 0.5.2

This release smooths several rough edges in the download and playlist workflows, improves accessibility, and tightens local-data error handling.

## What changed since 0.5.1

### Fixed workflow issues

- Fixed stale single-download inspection state. Editing the URL after checking a media item now clears the old inspection so a different URL cannot be saved with stale metadata.
- Fixed the same stale-state issue in Playlist mode. Editing a playlist URL now clears the old playlist inspection and selection before any save can be queued.
- Added a Remove action for queued playlist items that are still waiting, so unwanted items can be removed before they start downloading.
- Fixed playlist progress semantics. A playlist now shows 100% progress when every item has reached a terminal state, even if some items failed or were cancelled.

### Accessibility and polish

- Fixed broken tab ARIA relationships between navigation tabs and tab panels.
- Removed the focusable-but-invisible tab panel focus target.
- Connected URL validation/helper text to URL inputs with `aria-describedby`.
- Added `aria-pressed` to Audio/Video format toggles.
- Improved light-theme contrast for destructive badges and success metric text.
- Aligned the desktop app minimum window width with the desktop sidebar breakpoint so the app does not open in an awkward desktop/mobile hybrid layout.

### Local data handling

- Corrupted settings or Library JSON now surfaces a user-facing error instead of silently resetting preferences or showing an empty Library.
- Damaged local-data files are copied to a backup before the app asks the user to restore or remove them.

### Release assets

- `Unmuze_0.5.2_x64-setup.exe` - Windows NSIS installer.
- `Unmuze_0.5.2_x64_en-US.msi` - Windows MSI installer.
- `Unmuze_0.5.2_x64-portable.exe` - portable Windows executable.
- `SHA256SUMS.txt` - checksums for the uploaded artifacts.

## Verification

- `npm run check:release`
- `cargo test`
- Tauri release executable and Windows installer bundles were rebuilt for 0.5.2.

Note: signed Tauri updater metadata (`latest.json`) could not be generated in this local environment because `TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY` is not set.
